summary: H2D status must decode the active extruder's packed temperatures and the single-material AMS mapping instead of reporting inactive-nozzle and wrong-unit values.
reason: On dual-extruder reports, legacy top-level nozzle temperatures describe the inactive nozzle, while device.extruder carries packed per-nozzle values and active ID bits. Multi-AMS job mapping can encode unit and slot as separate bytes, so tray_now alone can select the same slot from the wrong AMS.
validated_outcome: A live H2D Studio print decoded active nozzle 1 at 220/220 C and mapping 258 as AMS unit 1 slot 2, matching the blue PLA Basic job instead of inactive nozzle 0 and unit 0 PC.
